content:
Member-only story

The hard part is not the question. The hard part is admitting that being stuck is no longer a private problem.

13 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why Asking for Help Feels Harder Than Doing the Work AloneAsking for help should be simple.
You are blocked. Someone else may know the answer. You ask. The problem moves forward.
That is how it sounds in theory.
In real engineering work, it rarely feels that clean.
You sit with the bug a little longer. You reread the error. You add another log. You search the codebase again. You restart the server. You try one more branch. You rewrite the function. You inspect the request. You test a different payload. You convince yourself you are close.
Sometimes you are.
Sometimes you are just protecting your ego with activity.
That is the uncomfortable truth about asking for help as a developer. The work is not always the thing we are avoiding. Sometimes the work feels easier than the conversation.
